perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information includes, without limitation, non -public information relating to released or unreleased Disclosing Party software pro ducts, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party’s business policies or practices, financial information, technical information, computer systems, infrastructure designs, data, analysis, compilations, studies or other doc umentation and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party, its related entities and/or agents is covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information includes non -public information relating to released or unreleased Disclosing Party software pro ducts, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party’s business policies or practices, financial information. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party, its related entities and/or agents is covered by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'technical information, computer systems, infrastructure designs, data, analysis, compilations, studies or other documentation and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential' from the definition of Confidential Information, but maintaining the statement that 'Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party, its related entities and/or agents is covered by this Agreement', creates uncertainty regarding what information is actually protected under the agreement. The in-text contradiction arises because the second sentence implies a broader scope of confidential information than the narrowed definition now provides.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information means nonpublic information that disclosing party (“Disclosing Party”) designates as being confidential or which, under the circumstances surrounding di sclosure the receiving party (“Receiving Party”) should know is treated as confidential by the Disclosing Party. Confidential Information includes non -public information relating to released or unreleased Disclosing Party software pro ducts, the marketing or promotion of any Disclosing Party product, Disclosing Party’s business policies or practices, financial information. Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party, its related entities and/or agents is covered by this Agreement. C onfidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is or subsequently becomes publicly available without Receiving Party’s breach of any obligation owed to Disclosing Party; (ii) became known to Receiving Party prior to Disclosing Party’s disclosure of such information to Receiving Party; (iii) became known to Receiving Party from a source other than Disclosing Party other than by the breach of an obligation of confidentiali ty owed to Disclosing Party; (iv) is independently developed by Rec eiving Party without access to the Disclosing Party’s information , or (v ) the Confidential Information is required to be disclosed pursuant to a requirement of a governmental agency or law so long as the other party is provided notice of such requirement p rior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' more broadly, including 'technical information, computer systems, infrastructure designs, data, analysis, compilations, studies or other documentation and information received from others that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential.' The changed text removes this specific list, narrowing the definition. The contradicted text also contains the broad definition along with the statement that 'Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party by any Disclosing Party, its related entities and/or agents is covered by this Agreement.' By narrowing the definition in the changed text while maintaining this sentence, a contradiction is created regarding the scope of protected information. This contradiction falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language was removed creating uncertainty and potential conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees disclose Confidential Inform ation only to those employees who need to know such information and certifies that such employees have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantia lly similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees disclose Confidential Inform ation only to those employees who need to know such information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and certifies that such employees have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantia lly similar to those of this Agreement', the text now contradicts the purpose of the NDA, since there is no guarantee or security to ensure that employees will respect the conditions of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that it shall not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person, firm, or business, except to the extent necessary for the T ransaction. The existence of any business negotiations, discussions, consultations, or agreements in progress between the parties shall not be released to any form of public media, unless agreed between the parties in writing. Both parties acknowledge that the Receiving Party shall protect the secrecy of all Confidential Information, that said Confidential Information is of critical importance to the Disclosing Party, that any violation of this Agreement would seriously and irreparably impair and damage the Disclosing Party's business, and that the Recipient shall keep all Confidential Information in a fiduciary capacity for the sole benefit of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information, and the Receiving Party represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees disclose Confidential Inform ation only to those employees who need to know such information. The Receiving Party will immediately and unconditionally give written notice to the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees to assist the Dis closing Party in remedying any such unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information. MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT 2
perturbation.justification: The original text included a certification that employees accessing Confidential Information have agreed to be bound by similar terms. The changed text removes this certification. The contradicted text emphasizes the importance of protecting Confidential Information and the Receiving Party's responsibility to do so. By removing the certification, the changed text weakens the protection of Confidential Information and thus contradicts the overall intent and specific obligations outlined in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict and weakening the agreement. This follows the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confide ntial Information at Disclosing Party’s request, or at Disclosing Party’s option, certify destruction of the same.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confide ntial Information at Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or at Disclosing Party’s option, certify destruction of the same', The Disclosing Party loses control over what to do with sensitive information after the agreement is over, that is if they want to destoy the information instead of receiving it. Now it introduces an in-text contradiction, limiting the options the Disclosing Party has.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Information Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confide ntial Information at Disclosing Party’s request. 4. Injunctive Relief Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confident ial Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive and other equitable relief (without bond and without the necessity of showing actual monetary damages) as may be deemed proper by a court.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Disclosing Party the option to have the Receiving Party either return or destroy the Confidential Information. The changed text removes the option for the Disclosing Party to have the Receiving Party destroy the information, limiting the Disclosing Party's control. While the contradicted text is similar to the changed text, the change causes a contradiction because the original agreement allows two options, and the new version removes one of them. By limiting the options of the Disclosing Party, a strong contradiction is introduced. Therefore, the change causes an in-text contradiction as described in the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confident ial Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive and other equitable relief (without bond and without the necessity of showing actual monetary damages) as may be deemed proper by a court.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confident ial Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive and other equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(without bond and without the necessity of showing actual monetary damages)', the changed text implies that the Disclosing Party *would* need to post a bond and *would* need to show actual monetary damages to obtain injunctive relief, which increases the burden of proof and cost to the disclosing party, creating an in-text contradiction with the goal of protecting confidential information quickly and efficiently.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confident ial Information and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive and other equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court. 5. No Further Rights All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed to as granting or conferring any rights in the Confidential Information except as provide d herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes '(without bond and without the necessity of showing actual monetary damages)' from the original text, which implies that the Disclosing Party now needs to post a bond and show actual monetary damages to obtain injunctive relief. This directly contradicts the intention of the clause in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, as both were originally intended to allow the Disclosing Party to obtain injunctive relief quickly and efficiently without needing to post a bond or show monetary damages. Therefore, the change creates an in-text contradiction by increasing the burden of proof and cost to the disclosing party.

perturbation.original_text: Any estimates or forecasts provided by either party to the other shall not constitute commitments.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the sentence 'Any estimates or forecasts provided by either party to the other shall not constitute commitments.' introduces a subtle but significant ambiguity. Without this disclaimer, estimates or forecasts exchanged during business discussions could be interpreted as commitments, especially if they are used as a basis for further action. This contradiction arises because the agreement aims to facilitate open discussions ('desire to engage in business related discussions and negotiations'), but without the sentence, any shared estimates or forecasts carry the risk of being misinterpreted as binding, which potentially hinders free and open discussion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, DBT and XXX (the “Parties”) desire to engage in business related discussions and negotiations regarding a potential business relationship (“Transaction”). WHEREAS, the Parties may provide to each other certain confidential and proprietary information in connection with the Transaction and each desires that any such information provided shall be k ept confidential by the other party; and WHEREAS, in consideration of the disclosure of such information, each party is willing to keep the other party’s information confidential in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, DBT and XXX hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text is empty, meaning the sentence 'Any estimates or forecasts provided by either party to the other shall not constitute commitments.' has been removed. The contradicted text states that the parties 'desire to engage in business related discussions and negotiations'. The explanation states that removing the original sentence means that estimates or forecasts exchanged during these discussions could be interpreted as commitments, hindering free and open discussion and thus contradicting the stated desire for open discussion in the contradicted text. This creates a strong, direct conflict regarding the interpretation of estimates and forecasts, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: None of the provisions of this Agre ement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, its agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized officer of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: None of the provisions of this Agre ement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, its agents, or employees.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized officer of Disclosing Party', The text becomes contradictory to the rest of the contract. Without this, provisions could be unintentionally waived through actions of the Disclosing Party, even without a formal written agreement. This introduces uncertainty and makes the agreement more susceptible to misinterpretation and disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constit utes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. None of the provisions of this Agre ement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, its agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized officer of Disclosing Party. No waiver of any provision of this Ag reement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. II. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the State of Massachusetts and both parties further consent to jurisdiction by the state an d federal courts sitting in Boston , Massachusetts .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a waiver of the agreement's provisions must be in writing and signed by an authorized officer. The changed text removes this requirement, meaning a waiver could be implied through actions or acquiescence. The contradicted text includes the original, stricter clause about waivers requiring a signed written instrument, creating a direct conflict. Now, one part of the agreement says a waiver requires a signed document, while another suggests it does not. The explanation provided indicates that this creates a contradiction. Therefore, the modification creates an in-text contradiction.
